content.claim: Expert interpretation of rising autism registrations attributes increase primarily to diagnostic-practice change—improved detection methods, greater awareness, and earlier referral by parents and professionals—rather than true prevalence increase
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Expert claim is quoted opinion rather than epidemiological evidence
No population-representative screening study cited to adjudicate between true-prevalence and ascertainment-bias explanations
Attribution to 'autism knowledge' and diagnostic spread is plausible but not directly validated by cited sources
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
